title: 10 best herbal essential oils for gum pain
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for gum pain are a natural and effective way to alleviate discomfort and inflammation in the mouth. These oils, extracted from plants such as tea tree, peppermint, and eucalyptus, possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm irritated gums. For instance, tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties combat bacteria that can cause gum disease, while peppermint oil's cooling sensation relieves pain and reduces swelling. By incorporating these oils into oral care routines, individuals with gum pain can experience improved mouth health, reduced discomfort, and enhanced overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with gum pain because it has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can effectively soothe and calm the affected area. When applied topically to the gums, peppermint oil's menthol content numbs the pain, reducing swelling and discomfort caused by gum inflammation or irritation. Additionally, peppermint oil's cooling sensation helps to desensitize the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving gum pain and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic properties: Peppermint essential oil's antiseptic properties help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the gums, which can exacerbate gum pain.
Anesthetic properties: The anesthetic properties of peppermint oil numb the affected area, reducing sensitivity and discomfort associated with gum pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that promote inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing menthol and other bioactive compounds, peppermint roots are effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with gum disease.
Buds: The young buds of the peppermint plant contain concentrated amounts of menthone, which helps to numb and soothe gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a healthy plant with no signs of pests or diseases for optimal quality.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator on the lowest setting for 24 hours to prevent spoilage.
Combine 50 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 72 hours to allow for maximum extraction of beneficial compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer the resulting essential oil to dark glass bottles for storage and use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which work to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The oil's antibacterial qualities also help combat gum infections and inflammation, promoting healthy gums and teeth. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects can soothe irritated gums and mouths, providing relief from sensitivity and pain associated with gum disease or dental procedures.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic property: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has antiseptic properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the gums, alleviating pain caused by gum disease or injury.

Analgesic property: Lemon balm essential oil contains compounds that exhibit analgesic properties, providing temporary relief from gum pain and discomfort associated with conditions like toothaches, abscesses, or wisdom teeth extraction.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in herbal lemon balm essential oil help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for gum inflammation and related discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemon balm roots help soothe gum inflammation and reduce pain associated with gum disease.
Leaves: Abundant in vitamin C and flavonoids, lemon balm leaves provide a gentle and effective remedy for reducing gum inflammation and promoting healthy gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of melissa officinalis leaves at peak flowering stage on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer under indirect sunlight or low-temperature fan circulation.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves using a water-to-material ratio of 1:4 for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid and refrigerate at 40-45°f (4-7°c) for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums. The oil's antibacterial compounds also work to combat gingivitis-causing bacteria, promoting healthy gums and preventing further irritation. Additionally, sage's analgesic properties help to numb the area, providing immediate relief from toothache pain and soreness. By applying topically or inhaling the oil, patients can experience significant reduction in gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Sage essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help to reduce bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth, which can contribute to gum pain and inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory property: Sage essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with gum pain and inflammation.
Analgesic property: Sage essential oil has analgesic properties that help to numb the area affected by gum pain, providing temporary relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm gums affected by pain.
Leaves: Contain salviaflavone, a flavonoid that has been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that can contribute to gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia officinalis leaves in quantities of 50g for best results.
Dry the leaves using air circulation method at room temperature for 3-4 days.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle grinder.
Steam distill the powdered herb mixture with water using a clevenger apparatus at 100°c for 1 hour.
Separate and filter the obtained essential oil from water using paper filters and glass pipettes within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Nutmeg essential oils helps with gum pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like myristicin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, providing relief from swelling, redness, and soreness. Additionally, nutmeg essential oils have natural analgesic properties that help numb the affected area, making it an effective natural remedy for managing gum pain caused by toothaches, cavities, or gum disease.
remedy_properties: Analgesic property: Nutmeg essential oil has analgesic properties, which help to numb the affected area and reduce gum pain by blocking the production of pain-causing compounds in the body.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compound present in nutmeg essential oil helps to reduce swelling and inflammation caused by gum disease or other oral health issues, thereby alleviating gum pain.
Antimicrobial property: Nutmeg essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help to combat bacterial growth and infection, which are common causes of gum pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, nutmeg roots help alleviate gum pain by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Rhyzomes: With their unique bioactive compounds, nutmeg rhyzomes provide analgesic properties that ease gum pain and discomfort.
Buds: Nutmeg buds contain potent volatile oils that soothe gum inflammation, reduce swelling, and promote a healthy mouth environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh myristica fragrans seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for about 30 seconds.
Combine the powdered myristica fragrans with 250 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days allowing the seeds to infuse and release their active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids after about 10 minutes.
Allow the infused carrier oil to mature at room temperature for an additional 7-10 days before filtering it again and using as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil, extracted from the leaves of the Eucalyptus globulus tree, contains compounds that effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. Additionally, the menthol and eucalyptol present in the oil provide a numbing effect, relieving pain and sensitivity associated with gum conditions such as gingivitis and periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums caused by conditions such as gingivitis or periodontitis.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil can help to combat bacteria that cause gum infections and inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing pain and discomfort.

Analgesic: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has analgesic properties, which means it can help to numb the pain in the gums, providing relief from acute or chronic pain caused by dental issues such as toothaches, abscesses, or gum surgery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are used to produce an essential oil that helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the gums.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian blue gum plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective for treating gum infections and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves and stems in the early morning after dew has dried off.
Dry the freshly harvested plant material using a clean drying rack or desiccant for 7-10 days.
Steam distill 100g of dried eucalyptus globulus plant material with 500ml of distilled water for 2 hours to obtain essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, protected from light and heat, for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's citral content has been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums, providing relief from toothaches and gum recession. Additionally, lemongrass oil's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that can cause gum disease, promoting a healthy oral environment. Its soothing aroma also calms the mind and mouth, reducing stress and anxiety that may exacerbate gum pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemongrass essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from gum pain caused by conditions like gingivitis or periodontitis.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of lemongrass essential oil help to combat bacteria that can cause infections in the gums, leading to pain and discomfort.
Analgesic: Lemongrass essential oil has a mild analgesic effect, which helps to numb the pain caused by gum inflammation or infection, providing temporary relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots are used to create a calming and anti-inflammatory oil that helps to reduce gum swelling and alleviate pain.
Stems: Lemongrass stems contain bioactive compounds that help to combat bacterial growth and inflammation in the mouth, reducing gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopopgon citratus leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Wash the harvested leaves gently with lukewarm water and mild soap to remove dirt and impurities.
Steam distill 20 grams of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves with 100 milliliters of distilled water at 100°c for 30 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the herbal cymbopogon citratus essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The oil's analgesic properties also help to numb the affected area, providing relief from shooting pains and sensitivity. Additionally, fennel's antibacterial qualities help to combat infections that can cause gum pain, promoting healthy oral flora and a decrease in inflammation. Overall, fennel essential oils provide a natural remedy for soothing and calming gum pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and redness in gums, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with gum inflammation.
Antimicrobial properties: Fennel essential oil's antimicrobial components combat bacterial infections that can cause gum disease, reducing the risk of further inflammation and pain.

Anesthetic properties: Fennel essential oil contains compounds that have a numbing effect on the gums, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort associated with gum irritation or inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving gum pain and inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the fennel plant contain bioactive compounds that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated gums, providing relief from gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Mix the seeds with 400 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for seven days to allow for infusion and gentle extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids after 15 minutes.
Allow the infused oil to mature for at least three weeks before bottling and using topically on gum tissue.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The oil's antimicrobial abilities also combat bacteria that can cause infection and exacerbate pain. Additionally, pot marigold's analgesic and antispasmodic properties provide relief from toothaches and gum sensitivity. By applying a few drops of pot marigold essential oils to the affected area or massaging it into the gums, individuals may experience significant alleviation of gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Calendula oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums, providing instant relief from gum pain.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy gums.
Wound healing: Calendula oil's regenerative properties accelerate wound healing, helping to soothe and repair damaged tissue in the mouth, including gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Flowers: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to combat bacteria and viruses that cause gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at dawn on a dry day, avoiding any signs of wilting or discoloration.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator set at 35°c for 8 hours, or air drying for 24 hours.
Combine 50 grams of dried calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate oil extraction and infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the resulting essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with gum pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, thyme oil reduces inflammation and swelling in the gums, providing relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum pain, promoting a healthy environment for teeth and gums to thrive.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Thyme essential oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause gum infections and inflammation, reducing pain and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and ease gum pain by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory compounds in the body.
Anesthetic: Thyme essential oil contains a compound called thymol, which has analgesic and anesthetic properties, providing temporary relief from gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are good for making medicinal essential oils because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help relieve gum pain.

Flowers: Thyme flowers are used to make essential oils for gum pain as they contain flavonoids and terpenes that have antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the early morning dew for optimal potency.
Wash the plant material gently with cold distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill the thymus vulgaris plant material using a glass still and 30% ethanol carrier oil at 100°c for 90 minutes.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation mixture using a separating funnel and transfer it to a dark glass bottle for storage.
Store the finished essential oil in a cool dark place for up to six months or use immediately for maximum potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Basil essential oils helps with gum pain because it possesses natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can effectively soothe and calm irritated gums. The oil's antibacterial qualities also help combat bacteria that cause gingivitis and gum infections, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, basil's ability to reduce swelling and ease muscle tension helps alleviate pain associated with gum recession, making it an effective natural remedy for gum-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Basil essential oil has antibacterial properties that can help combat bacterial infections in the gums, reducing inflammation and pain associated with gum disease.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Basil essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving gum pain.

Anesthetic and Analgesic Properties: Basil essential oil contains compounds that have anesthetic and analgesic properties, which can help numb the pain in the gums, providing temporary relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Basil roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate gum pain and swelling.
Buds: The buds of the basil plant contain high concentrations of eugenol, a natural analgesic that provides relief from toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh ocimum basilicum leaves on a sunny day in july.
Steam distill the harvested leaves with 500ml of water for 2 hours and 15 minutes.
Filter the resulting oil through cheesecloth and collect it in a dark glass bottle.
Store the essential oil in an airtight container at room temperature for 14 days.
Dilute the essential oil with 10% carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for topical use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum pain with herbal essential oils is to combine a few drops of peppermint, tea tree, and clove oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Apply the mixture to your gums using a cotton swab or toothbrush, holding for 30 seconds before spitting it out. Repeat twice daily for best results, as this blend helps reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and ease discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum pain that are treated with herbal essential oils are headaches, toothaches, and mouth sores. Peppermint oil is often used to relieve headache discomfort, while lavender oil can help soothe toothaches and reduce inflammation. Chamomile oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease mouth soreness and irritations. Additionally, ginger oil is known for its analgesic properties, which can provide relief from pain associated with gum disease or other oral health issues.
